key_insight: PF-DIC extends digital image correlation with a phase-field damage model so it can measure displacement, strain, and crack evolution in a single framework.
engineering_relevance: It enables more accurate full-field measurement near cracks, reduces the need for manual crack masks, and supports automated damage assessment for structural health monitoring.
method: Embed a phase-field variable into the DIC optimization to represent damage and complex crack morphology.
Solve for full-field displacement and strain while simultaneously identifying crack paths from speckle images.
Use a finite element implementation to handle cracked samples and selectively detect critical cracks under different loading conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical examples showing that PF-DIC captures different crack types and improves displacement accuracy, especially near crack tips.
watch_item: Performance will depend on the quality of the phase-field formulation and finite element setup, and robustness on highly noisy experimental images is not established in the abstract.
paper_title: PF-DIC: Phase field digital image correlation for integrated full-field displacement, strain, and damage measurements
